section.heading: Definitions
section.chapeau: In this subchapter, the following definitions apply:

paragraph.heading: Child abuse and neglect.—
paragraph.content: The term “child abuse and neglect” has the meaning given that term in section 3 of the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act (42 U.S.C. 5101 note).

paragraph.heading: Child development center employee.—
paragraph.content: The term “child development center employee” means a civilian employee of the Coast Guard who is employed to work in a Coast Guard child development center without regard to whether the employee is paid from appropriated or nonappropriated funds.

paragraph.heading: Coast guard child development center.—
paragraph.content: The term “Coast Guard child development center” means a facility on Coast Guard property or on property under the jurisdiction of the commander of a Coast Guard unit at which child care services are provided for members of the Coast Guard.

paragraph.heading: Competitive service position.—
paragraph.content: The term “competitive service position” means a position in the competitive service (as defined in section 2102 of title 5).

paragraph.heading: Family home daycare.—
paragraph.chapeau: The term “family home daycare” means home-based child care services provided for a member of the Coast Guard by an individual who—

subparagraph.content: is certified by the Commandant as qualified to provide home-based child care services; and
provides home-based child care services on a regular basis in exchange for monetary compensation.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 3 of the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act, referred to in par. (1), is section 3 of Pub. L. 93–247, which is set out as a note under section 5101 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Pub. L. 115–282 renumbered section 551 of this title as this section.
